picks.rationale: AI data-center capex is accelerating and remains supply-constrained in memory, optics, and power — sustaining pricing and unit growth across GPU platforms. HBM remains tight, extending the compute cycle's duration and margin support.
picks.variant_perception: Street underestimates the length of the AI buildout and NVIDIA's mix shift to high-ASP platforms and software/networking attach, keeping gross margins structurally higher for longer.

picks.rationale: AWS re-acceleration driven by AI workloads and enterprise cloud migrations. Advertising segment growing at 20%+ with near-100% incremental margins. Retail profitability inflecting as logistics network matures.
picks.variant_perception: Market discounts the durability of AWS margin expansion and the advertising flywheel. FCF inflection is underappreciated relative to earnings power — free cash flow yield is materially higher than reported earnings suggest.

picks.rationale: Azure cloud + Office Copilot upsell creates a durable margin flywheel. Strong FCF and reasonable forward multiple given growth trajectory. One of the cleanest ways to own the AI transition with lower execution risk than pure-play names.
picks.variant_perception: Consensus underestimates Copilot attach rates in enterprise — early renewal data suggests AI seat upsell is stickier than legacy software expansions, pointing to durable ARPU expansion through the decade.
